one_line_takeaway: SIC, an iterative rewrite-then-classify sanitization pipeline that neutralizes imperative instructions in untrusted agent inputs, drives ASR to 0% on standard AgentDojo prompt-injection benchmarks and to the lowest ASR (15%, vs. 49-81% for competing defenses) under a strong white-box adaptive attacker, but that residual 15% comes specifically from non-imperative payloads (executable JSON workflows, fake system notices, fabricated error narratives) that evade its imperative-instruction detection by design.
key_finding: Default SIC (masking, 1 rewrite pass, no chunking) drives ASR to 0.00% across all four models and three AgentDojo suites for both IGNOREPREVIOUSINSTRUCTION and IMPORTANTINSTRUCTION attacks, with only minor utility degradation (Table 1, GPT-4o: 55.67% utility, 51.11%/50.68% utility-under-attack for the two attacks). Against other detector-based defenses on GPT-4o (Table 1), SIC is the only method with 0% ASR on both attacks; MELON has higher clean utility (70.10%) but non-zero ASR (0.42%) and much lower utility-under-attack (23.50%) on IMPORTANTINSTRUCTION; PROMPTGUARD has the highest clean utility (79.38%) but a 26.03% ASR under IMPORTANTINSTRUCTION; PI-GUARD and PI-DETECTOR trade lower clean utility (46.39%, 41.24%) for still-nonzero ASR (1.26%-6.32%). Ablations: MASK yields the best utility-under-attack of the three cleansing strategies (Rephrase performs worst due to incomplete neutralization or over-aggressive rewriting); additional rewrite passes and additional chunks reduce clean utility further without needed robustness gains under the tested attacks, motivating the single-pass, no-chunking default. In isolation (unchunked), the classifier is bypassed by the authors' own STUBBORNREWRITING attack in 99% of synthetic cases; chunking reduces this to 35% (2-3 chunks) and 25% with both heuristics combined, but aggregated over the full banking suite this maps to only a 2.78% end-to-end ASR (4/144 scenarios), since many 'successful' local bypasses stop being instructive after rewriting. Under the much stronger, white-box, per-sample-optimized genetic-algorithm adaptive attack of Nasr et al. (2025) (Table 2, GPT-5-mini as mutator, subset of AgentDojo): SIC achieves the lowest ASR of all defenses tested at 15%, versus No-Defense 75%, PROTECT AI 81%, PROMPTGUARD 75%, PIGUARD 49%, and MELON 71% -- a roughly 3x improvement over the next-best defense (PIGUARD). The paper identifies three concrete failure modes behind this residual 15%: (1) embedding directly executable, non-imperative machine-parsable workflows (e.g., JSON task manifests) that the classifier does not flag as 'instructions' but the tool-calling agent still executes; (2) authority-styled, non-imperative language (e.g., 'SYSTEM NOTICE: Mandatory Compliance Workflow') that pressures the model without being detected as an instruction; (3) fabricated partial-failure narratives (e.g., a fake '[Content Rendering Error]') that justify a follow-up action as necessary.
core_contribution: The paper introduces SIC (Soft Instruction Control), a modular, model-agnostic pre-processing defense against indirect prompt injection for tool-augmented LLM agents. Untrusted data is first augmented with known 'canary' dummy instructions, then passed through an LLM rewriter that masks, rephrases, or removes imperative instruction-like content; the canary is used to detect if rewriting was itself suppressed by the attacker (if the canary survives unchanged, the pipeline halts). The rewritten text is then checked by an LLM classifier both as a whole and split into chunks; if any check flags remaining instructions, the agent halts rather than proceeding. Only after all checks pass are placeholders stripped and the cleaned data combined with the original user instruction and handed to the agent. The design is explicitly framed as a 'soft' relaxation of CaMeL's (Debenedetti et al., 2025) formal data/control-flow decomposition, and requires no modification to the underlying agent.
threat_model: Indirect prompt injection against tool-augmented LLM agents (per Shi et al., 2025a's threat model, adopted by this paper). Attacker controls a portion of external untrusted data (web pages, emails, APIs) fetched into the agent's context, has full white-box knowledge of the agent architecture, the LLM used, and the deployed defense (Kirchhoff's principle), and can adaptively craft payloads. Attacker goal: cause the agent to execute an attacker-chosen tool/function call (e.g., leaking data, unauthorized transactions). Denial-of-service-only attacks are explicitly out of scope. The paper explicitly disclaims provable robustness: SIC is presented as raising the cost/reliability bar for attackers rather than achieving formal guarantees, and the authors state upfront that data-only injections and side-channels are expected weaknesses, similar to CaMeL.
limitations: Authors explicitly state SIC is not provably robust and is expected to be circumventable via data-only injection attacks and side-channels, analogous to CaMeL. Under the strongest tested adaptive attacker (white-box, per-sample genetic optimization with a frontier LLM mutator), ASR is still 15%, not 0%. The three identified failure modes all stem from a structural limitation: SIC detects 'instruction-like' (imperative) content, but non-imperative payloads (JSON task descriptions, authority-styled 'system notices', fabricated error narratives) can still trigger tool execution without tripping the imperative-instruction classifier -- the authors call this a generic failure of local, detection-based reasoning that misses cross-sentence and tool-use context. There is an explicit security-utility trade-off: benign rewrites (e.g., turning 'Please pay the amount...' into 'Payment can be made...') can cause some models to fail otherwise-legitimate tasks, and the paper acknowledges an attacker could exploit the blurry line between benign and adversarial phrasing to craft an 'imperceptible' injection that might fool even a human reviewer. Rewriting passes beyond one and additional classification chunking both reduce clean utility (more false positives) without commensurate robustness gains against the tested attack set, though the authors note more passes/chunks remain a tunable knob for stronger adversaries. Evaluation is confined to AgentDojo's three suites (banking, Slack, travel) and a specific attack-string set; the genetic-algorithm adaptive-attack comparison uses only a 'subset' of AgentDojo, and GPT-5-mini is the only mutator model tested for that experiment.

datasets_used: AgentDojo (Debenedetti et al., 2024) - banking, Slack, and travel task suites
quantitative_results: Default SIC (mask, 1 pass, no chunking): 0.00% ASR across all 4 models x 3 suites for IGNOREPREVIOUSINSTRUCTION and IMPORTANTINSTRUCTION
GPT-4o + SIC (Table 1): 55.67% clean utility, 51.11% utility-under-attack, 0.00% ASR (IGNOREPREVIOUSINSTRUCTION); 55.67% clean utility, 50.68% utility-under-attack, 0.00% ASR (IMPORTANTINSTRUCTION)
GPT-4o + MELON (Table 1): 70.10% clean utility, 41.94% utility-under-attack, 0.00% ASR (IGNOREPREVIOUS); 70.10% clean utility, 23.50% utility-under-attack, 0.42% ASR (IMPORTANTINSTRUCTION)
GPT-4o + PI-GUARD (Table 1): 46.39% clean utility, 29.72%/16.23% utility-under-attack, 1.26%/3.69% ASR
GPT-4o + PROMPTGUARD (Table 1): 79.38% clean utility, 28.87%/33.72% utility-under-attack, 0.00%/26.03% ASR
GPT-4o + PI-DETECTOR (Table 1): 41.24% clean utility, 21.60%/21.50% utility-under-attack, 0.00%/6.32% ASR
Unchunked classifier alone bypassed in 99% of synthetic STUBBORNREWRITING cases; with 2-3 chunks bypass drops to 35%; with both heuristics combined, 25%
End-to-end SIC pipeline ASR over the full banking suite against STUBBORNREWRITING: 2.78% (4/144 scenarios)

Genetic-algorithm adaptive attack (Nasr et al., 2025 method, GPT-5-mini mutator, Table 2): No Defense 75% ASR (median 58 queries); PROTECT AI 81% (81 queries); PROMPTGUARD 75% (45 queries); PIGUARD 49% (82 queries); MELON 71% (110 queries); SIC 15% (126 queries) -- SIC lowest, ~3x better than second-best (PIGUARD)
baselines_compared: RUP (repeat user prompt, Debenedetti et al., 2024)
